message:
Add edge-case robustness tests for Aider, Amp, Cursor, Gemini, OpenCode, and PiAgent connectors

Extend each connector's test suite with malformed input coverage
(br-2w98), mirroring the edge-case tests already added for Claude Code
and Codex connectors. Tests cover:

- Truncated JSON/JSONL files (mid-JSON, mid-UTF-8)
- Invalid UTF-8 bytes gracefully skipped
- Empty and whitespace-only files return no conversations
- JSON type mismatches (string/number where array expected)
- Deeply nested JSON (200+ levels) handled without stack overflow
- Large message bodies (1MB+) processed without OOM
- Null bytes embedded in content
- UTF-8 BOM marker at file start
- Nonexistent file returns error
- Mixed \r\n and \n line endings (Aider)
- Bare quote markers with no content (Aider)
- Very long single lines (1M chars) handled (Aider)
- User-only and assistant-only message files (Aider)
- 100MB+ file skip mechanism (connectors with size limits)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
